system.docHtml:
Monophobia is an intense, irrational fear of being alone. People with monophobia may experience a range of symptoms when they think about, see, or are in situations where they are alone.

Impulse

Constantly seeking companionship and avoiding activities or situations where solitude is required. Abberance (ABE) increases by 1 from the source of fear.

Disorder

In addition to trait intensity behaviors: Overwhelming fear or panic at the thought of being by oneself. Abberance (ABE) increases by 3 (instead of 1) from the source of fear.

Physical Symptoms

Rapid heartbeat, sweating, trembling, shortness of breath, nausea, or dizziness.

Intrusive Thoughts

Persistent and distressing thoughts about abandonment, isolation, or lack of security.

Emotional Distress

Deep feelings of dread, loneliness, and helplessness when alone or contemplating solitude.
